description_long: This water cooler is a tall, rectangular appliance with a smooth, light gray exterior and a subtle green accent line running horizontally near the top. The front face features a recessed dispensing area with a tray and a dark panel, likely housing the spout and control interface. The sides are plain, with one side showing a visible power cord emerging from the lower back, indicating its electrical operation. The back is unadorned except for the cord entry point, and there is no visible water bottle installed on top. The overall design is modern and utilitarian, suitable for placement in public or semi-public urban environments such as offices, parks, or waiting areas. The surface appears to be made primarily of metal or high-grade plastic, with a hard, smooth finish.
description: A tall, rectangular water cooler with a smooth light gray body, green accent line, and a recessed dispensing area on the front. It features a power cord at the back and a modern, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the dispensing area with a tray and dark control panel, as well as the green accent line near the top.
description_view_1: Left side view is plain, with the power cord partially visible emerging from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the cord entry point at the bottom and an otherwise featureless back panel.
description_view_3: Right side view is plain, with the power cord more prominently visible curving outward.
attribute_dispenser_spout_type: recessed push-button spout integrated into a dark panel
attribute_control_panel_design: Single dark rectangular panel with likely push-buttons or touch controls, no visible indicators or displays
